chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Necessitates Discretionary Punishment, Legal Punishment, Stoning, Execution, and Exile in Adultery

text: 5030 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أُتِيَ بِرَجُلٍ أَصَابَ حَدّاً وَ بِهِ قُرُوحٌ فِي جَسَدِهِ كَثِيرَةٌ فَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "أَقِرُّوهُ حَتَّى يَبْرَأَ لاَ تَنْكَئُوهَا عَلَيْهِ فَتَقْتُلُوهُ" ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5030 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni:
It is reported that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) was brought a man who had committed an offense deserving of a legal punishment (hadd), and the man had numerous sores on his body.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "Delay the execution of the punishment until he recovers. Do not aggravate his sores, lest you kill him."
